title: senecio leptopodus
intro: Senecio leptopodus, also known as Blue Mountain ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a bitter, expectorant, and antispasmodic. The health benefits of Senecio leptopodus include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional remedies, such as cough medicines and salves.
